title: May-Flower.
content:
Pink, small, and punctual,
    Aromatic, low,
    Covert in April,
    Candid in May,

    Dear to the moss,
    Known by the knoll,
    Next to the robin
    In every human soul.

    Bold little beauty,
    Bedecked with thee,
    Nature forswears
    Antiquity.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "May-Flower."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
